solute.description: This molecule, 2-chlorophenylacetic acid, consists of a benzene ring substituted with a carboxymethyl group and a chlorine atom at the *ortho* (2-position) relative to the side chain. The primary functional groups are a terminal carboxylic acid and an aryl halide, which are separated by a single methylene bridge. It features a polar, acidic head capable of both donating and accepting hydrogen bonds, contrasted by a hydrophobic aromatic core. The molecule is relatively small and compact, with its physicochemical character defined by the localized electronegativity of the chlorine atom and the carboxylic acid group.

solvent.description: This molecule, ethylbenzene, consists of a six-membered aromatic benzene ring substituted with a two-carbon ethyl chain at a single position. It is a simple alkyl-substituted aromatic hydrocarbon characterized by a saturated ethyl group directly bonded to one of the sp2-hybridized carbons of the phenyl ring. The structure is nonpolar and lacks any hydrogen bond donors or acceptors, as it is composed entirely of carbon and hydrogen atoms. With a molecular formula of C8H10, it is a small molecule defined by the combination of a rigid, planar aromatic core and a flexible, short aliphatic side chain.
